opportunities.title: Consensys Hired a North Korean Dev and Didn't Know It — Neither Would You
opportunities.why_now: A16z-backed Consensys got infiltrated by DPRK operatives posing as contractors, proving even top-tier crypto firms can't vet their own supply chain.
opportunities.angle: Incident breakdown + checklist: the red flags every crypto company ignores when hiring remote devs

opportunities.title: Your Mac Isn't Safe: New Malware Hijacks Telegram to Drain Wallets
opportunities.why_now: SlowMist just disclosed active macOS malware specifically targeting crypto investors through Telegram session hijacking.
opportunities.angle: Threat explainer with concrete mitigation steps — session hygiene, 2FA, device compartmentalization for anyone holding keys

opportunities.title: Hardware Wallets Aren't 'Complete Garbage' — But ZachXBT Has a Point
opportunities.why_now: A live spat between ZachXBT and Trezor's exec over hardware wallet security is happening in public right now, forcing a real conversation about self-custody assumptions.
opportunities.angle: Balanced security teardown: where hardware wallets actually fail (supply chain, clipboard malware, physical attacks) vs where the criticism overreaches

opportunities.title: Ostium's Oracle Just Got Exploited for Millions — Here's the Playbook Attackers Keep Reusing
opportunities.why_now: Ostium paused trading after a fresh multimillion-dollar oracle exploit, the latest in a long line of oracle-manipulation hacks that keep getting priced in as 'surprises.'
opportunities.angle: Postmortem pattern-match: compare Ostium's exploit mechanics to past oracle hacks to show it was foreseeable

opportunities.title: SummerFi Is Dead. The Exploit That Killed It Should Have Killed the Protocol Model Too
opportunities.why_now: SummerFi is shutting down after seven years, explicitly citing an exploit, a rare case of a protocol admitting the hack was terminal rather than quietly rebranding.
opportunities.angle: Obituary/lessons piece: what SummerFi's collapse says about the sustainability of protocols that survive one hack but not the reputational bleed
